fixtures.description: One-dimensional rational linear flow with a threshold-triggered commitment update and a certified unique crossing bracket.
Two-dimensional computable globally Lipschitz nonlinear flow with certified interval refinement and no guards.
Piecewise-rational control and a declared finite event enumerator for isolated guard crossings, resets, and revision history.
A fixed finite time grid is asserted to be the exact continuous trajectory and misses an intervening guard crossing.
The representation invokes undeclared exact-real equality, missed-event search, and future-trajectory oracles.
A guard is known abstractly to have isolated crossings but supplies no computable finite enumerator, certified brackets, or effective separation data.
A non-Lipschitz initial-value problem with multiple solutions and no frozen selection rule.
A hybrid system with infinitely many events in finite time.
